description_long: This object consists of a cluster of elevated cabins, each supported by stilts and connected by narrow wooden walkways. The cabins feature simple, rectangular forms with corrugated blue-gray gable roofs and are constructed primarily from light-colored wood. The structures are raised above a dark, uneven ground plane, suggesting adaptation to wet or flood-prone environments. Each cabin has small, asymmetrically placed windows and doors, with some doors opening directly onto the connecting walkways. The overall arrangement is compact, with the cabins positioned close together, evoking a stilt village or informal settlement. The visible surfaces are matte and slightly rough, with the roofs providing a distinct visual contrast to the pale walls.
description: A group of stilted wooden cabins with blue-gray gable roofs, connected by narrow walkways over a dark ground. The cabins have small, scattered windows and doors, creating a rustic, elevated settlement.
description_view_0: Front view showing three cabins, their stilted bases, and the connecting walkways, with the largest cabin in the background.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the walkways between cabins and the staggered arrangement of the structures.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the back sides of the cabins and the elevated platforms, with fewer visible windows and doors.
description_view_3: Opposite front view showing the cabins from the other side, with clear visibility of the walkways and rooflines.
attribute_roof_type: gable roofs with corrugated metal panels and moderate overhangs
attribute_window_arrangement: multiple small, rectangular windows distributed asymmetrically across the cabin walls, with no clear pattern or grouping
attribute_door_placement: single doors on the front and side facades of each cabin, some opening directly onto connecting walkways
